dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Filter data for Item Code 10 and Manager John Jones
filtered_data = data[(data['Item Code'] == 10) & (data['Manager On Duty'] == 'John Jones')]

# Calculate total quantity
total_quantity = filtered_data['Quantity'].sum()

print(f"Total quantity of Item 10 sold while John Jones was Manager on Duty: {total_quantity}")

# Determine closest answer
answers = {'a': 300, 'b': 600, 'c': 900, 'd': 2000}
closest = min(answers.items(), key=lambda x: abs(x[1] - total_quantity))
print(f"Closest answer: {closest[0]} ({closest[1]})")

# Create visualization
if len(filtered_data) > 0:
    fig = px.histogram(filtered_data, x='Quantity', 
                      title='Distribution of Quantities for Item 10 (John Jones as Manager)',
                      labels={'Quantity': 'Quantity per Transaction', 'count': 'Frequency'})
    fig.update_xaxes(title_text='Quantity per Transaction')
    fig.update_yaxes(title_text='Number of Transactions')
    fig.show()
else:
    print("No data found for Item 10 with John Jones as manager")

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Group by Item Code and sum quantities
item_sales = data.groupby('Item Code')['Quantity'].sum().reset_index()
item_sales = item_sales.sort_values('Quantity', ascending=False)

# Get top 3 highest selling items
top_3_items = item_sales.head(3)['Item Code'].tolist()
print(f"Top 3 highest selling items by quantity: {top_3_items}")
print("\nDetailed breakdown:")
for i, row in item_sales.head(3).iterrows():
    print(f"Item {int(row['Item Code'])}: {int(row['Quantity'])} units")

# Check against answer choices
answer_choices = {
    'a': [8, 17, 40],
    'b': [4, 11, 49],
    'c': [6, 17, 18],
    'd': [7, 11, 33]
}

for choice, items in answer_choices.items():
    if set(top_3_items) == set(items):
        print(f"\nAnswer: {choice}. {items}")
        break

# Visualization
fig = px.bar(item_sales.head(10), x='Item Code', y='Quantity',
             title='Top 10 Items by Total Quantity Sold')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Item Code')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Quantity Sold')
fig.show()

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Group by Sales Person and Item Code to get cumulative quantities
sales_summary = data.groupby(['Sales Person', 'Item Code']).agg({'Quantity': 'sum'}).reset_index()

# Find the maximum quantity sold by any salesperson for a single item
max_quantity_idx = sales_summary['Quantity'].idxmax()
max_row = sales_summary.loc[max_quantity_idx]

print(f"Highest cumulative quantity of a single item:")
print(f"Sales Person: {max_row['Sales Person']}")
print(f"Item Code: {int(max_row['Item Code'])}")
print(f"Total Quantity: {int(max_row['Quantity'])}")

# Get top 10 for visualization
top_10 = sales_summary.nlargest(10, 'Quantity')
top_10['Label'] = top_10['Sales Person'] + ' - Item ' + top_10['Item Code'].astype(int).astype(str)

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(top_10, x='Label', y='Quantity', 
             title='Top 10 Salesperson-Item Combinations by Quantity Sold',
             color='Quantity', color_continuous_scale='viridis')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Salesperson - Item Code', tickangle=45)
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Quantity Sold')
fig.show()

# Check answer options
print("\nAnswer verification:")
for person in ['Kelly', 'Sally']:
    for item in [17, 20]:
        qty = sales_summary[(sales_summary['Sales Person'].str.contains(person)) & 
                           (sales_summary['Item Code'] == item)]['Quantity'].sum()
        print(f"{person}, Item {item}: {qty}")

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Calculate total sales for each record
data['Total_Sales'] = data['Sales Price Per Unit'] * data['Quantity']

# Filter for Wendel's sales and calculate total
wendel_sales = data[data['Sales Person'] == 'Wendel Westborne']['Total_Sales'].sum()
print(f"Wendel Westborne's total sales: ${wendel_sales:,.2f}")

# Calculate total sales by salesperson for comparison
sales_by_person = data.groupby('Sales Person')['Total_Sales'].sum().reset_index()
sales_by_person = sales_by_person.sort_values('Total_Sales', ascending=False)

print("\nSales by all salespeople:")
for _, row in sales_by_person.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['Sales Person']}: ${row['Total_Sales']:,.2f}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(sales_by_person, x='Sales Person', y='Total_Sales', 
             title='Total Sales by Salesperson',
             labels={'Total_Sales': 'Total Sales ($)', 'Sales Person': 'Salesperson'})
fig.update_xaxes(title='Salesperson')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Sales ($)', tickformat='$,.0f')
fig.show()

# Answer determination
if wendel_sales < 22500:
    answer = 'a. $15,000'
elif wendel_sales < 165000:
    answer = 'b. $30,000'
elif wendel_sales < 400000:
    answer = 'c. $300,000'
else:
    answer = 'd. $500,000'

print(f"\nClosest answer: {answer}")

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date to datetime
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])

# Filter for May 2013 and Item Code 5
may_item5 = data[(data['Date'].dt.month == 5) & (data['Date'].dt.year == 2013) & (data['Item Code'] == 5)]

# Group by postal code and sum quantities
postal_quantities = may_item5.groupby('Postal Code')['Quantity'].sum().reset_index()
postal_quantities = postal_quantities.sort_values('Quantity', ascending=False)

print("May 2013 - Item 5 quantities by postal code:")
for _, row in postal_quantities.iterrows():
    print(f"Postal Code {int(row['Postal Code'])}: {int(row['Quantity'])} units")

top_postal = int(postal_quantities.iloc[0]['Postal Code'])
print(f"\nAnswer: Postal code {top_postal} bought the most Item 5 in May")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(postal_quantities, x='Postal Code', y='Quantity', 
             title='Item 5 Sales by Postal Code - May 2013',
             labels={'Quantity': 'Total Quantity Sold'})
fig.update_xaxes(title='Postal Code', tickmode='linear')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Quantity Sold')
fig.show()

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date column to datetime
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])

# Filter for February 2013
feb_data = data[data['Date'].dt.month == 2]

# Group by postal code and sum quantities
postal_summary = feb_data.groupby('Postal Code')['Quantity'].sum().reset_index()
postal_summary.columns = ['Postal Code', 'Total Quantity']

# Find postal codes with more than 400 products
high_volume_codes = postal_summary[postal_summary['Total Quantity'] > 400]
count_over_400 = len(high_volume_codes)

print(f"Postal codes that bought more than 400 products in February: {count_over_400}")
print("\nDetailed breakdown:")
for _, row in high_volume_codes.iterrows():
    print(f"Postal Code {int(row['Postal Code'])}: {int(row['Total Quantity'])} products")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(postal_summary, x='Postal Code', y='Total Quantity', 
             title='Total Quantity Purchased by Postal Code in February 2013',
             color='Total Quantity', color_continuous_scale='viridis')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Postal Code')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Quantity')
fig.add_hline(y=400, line_dash='dash', line_color='red', 
              annotation_text='400 products threshold')
fig.show()

print(f"\nAnswer: {count_over_400} postal codes bought more than 400 products in February")

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Filter for postal code 3020
postcode_3020 = data[data['Postal Code'] == 3020].copy()

# Calculate total dollars spent per line item
postcode_3020['Total_Spent'] = postcode_3020['Sales Price Per Unit'] * postcode_3020['Quantity']

# Group by item code and sum total spending
item_spending = postcode_3020.groupby('Item Code')['Total_Spent'].sum().reset_index()

# Sort by total spending and get top 3
top_3_items = item_spending.sort_values('Total_Spent', ascending=False).head(3)

print("Top 3 items by spending for Postal Code 3020:")
for idx, row in top_3_items.iterrows():
    print(f"Item {int(row['Item Code'])}: ${row['Total_Spent']:.2f}")

top_3_codes = top_3_items['Item Code'].astype(int).tolist()
print(f"\nTop 3 item codes: {top_3_codes}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(top_3_items, x='Item Code', y='Total_Spent', 
             title='Top 3 Items by Total Spending - Postal Code 3020',
             labels={'Total_Spent': 'Total Dollars Spent', 'Item Code': 'Item Code'})

fig.update_xaxes(title='Item Code')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Dollars Spent ($)')
fig.show()

# Check answer options
if set(top_3_codes) == {6, 9, 46}:
    print("Answer: a. 6, 9, 46")
elif set(top_3_codes) == {3, 6, 9}:
    print("Answer: b. 3, 6, 9")
elif set(top_3_codes) == {7, 20, 28}:
    print("Answer: c. 7, 20, 28")
elif set(top_3_codes) == {6, 9, 44}:
    print("Answer: d. 6, 9, 44")

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date column to datetime
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])

# Filter for May 2013
may_data = data[(data['Date'].dt.month == 5) & (data['Date'].dt.year == 2013)]

# Count unique invoices per salesperson
invoice_counts = may_data.groupby('Sales Person')['Invoice Number'].nunique().reset_index()
invoice_counts.columns = ['Sales Person', 'Invoice Count']

# Sort by invoice count descending
invoice_counts = invoice_counts.sort_values('Invoice Count', ascending=False)

print("Sales Person Rankings (May 2013 - by number of invoices):")
for i, row in invoice_counts.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['Sales Person']}: {row['Invoice Count']} invoices")

# Extract ranking order
ranking = invoice_counts['Sales Person'].str.split().str[0].tolist()
print(f"\nRanking order: {', '.join(ranking)}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(invoice_counts, x='Sales Person', y='Invoice Count', 
             title='Number of Invoices by Sales Person (May 2013)')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Sales Person')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Number of Invoices')
fig.show()

# Check against options
options = {
    'a': ['Kelly', 'Sally', 'Amy', 'Wendel', 'Benny'],
    'b': ['Kelly', 'Wendel', 'Amy', 'Sally', 'Benny'],
    'c': ['Kelly', 'Amy', 'Wendel', 'Sally', 'Benny'],
    'd': ['Kelly', 'Amy', 'Sally', 'Benny', 'Wendel']
}

for option, order in options.items():
    if ranking == order:
        print(f"\nAnswer: {option}")
        break

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Calculate revenue per line item
data['Revenue'] = data['Sales Price Per Unit'] * data['Quantity']

# Group by invoice and sales person to get total revenue per invoice
invoice_revenue = data.groupby(['Invoice Number', 'Sales Person'])['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()

# Filter for Wendel Westborne
wendel_invoices = invoice_revenue[invoice_revenue['Sales Person'] == 'Wendel Westborne']

# Find the largest invoice by revenue
largest_invoice = wendel_invoices.loc[wendel_invoices['Revenue'].idxmax()]
print(f"Wendel's largest invoice by revenue:")
print(f"Invoice Number: {largest_invoice['Invoice Number']:.0f}")
print(f"Revenue: ${largest_invoice['Revenue']:.2f}")

# Create visualization of Wendel's top 10 invoices
top_10 = wendel_invoices.nlargest(10, 'Revenue')
fig = px.bar(top_10, x='Invoice Number', y='Revenue', 
             title="Wendel's Top 10 Invoices by Revenue")
fig.update_xaxes(title='Invoice Number')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Revenue ($)')
fig.show()

# Answer verification
answer_options = [143, 2166, 3269, 3327]
if largest_invoice['Invoice Number'] in answer_options:
    print(f"\nAnswer: {chr(97 + answer_options.index(largest_invoice['Invoice Number']))}. {largest_invoice['Invoice Number']:.0f}")
else:
    print(f"\nInvoice {largest_invoice['Invoice Number']:.0f} not in options")

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Calculate total discount amount per transaction
data['Total_Discount'] = data['Discount Given'] * data['Quantity']

# Group by sales person and sum total discounts
discount_by_person = data.groupby('Sales Person')['Total_Discount'].sum().reset_index()

# Sort by total discount descending
discount_by_person = discount_by_person.sort_values('Total_Discount', ascending=False)

# Create bar chart
fig = px.bar(discount_by_person, 
             x='Sales Person', 
             y='Total_Discount',
             title='Total Dollar Value of Discounts Given by Sales Person',
             labels={'Total_Discount': 'Total Discount Amount ($)'})

fig.update_xaxes(title='Sales Person')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Discount Amount ($)')
fig.show()

# Print ranking
print('Ranking of sales persons by discount amount (most to least):')
for i, row in discount_by_person.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['Sales Person']}: ${row['Total_Discount']:.2f}")

# Extract names for answer comparison
ranking = discount_by_person['Sales Person'].str.split().str[0].tolist()
print(f'\nRanking order: {", ".join(ranking)}')

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Convert Date to datetime and extract month
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])
data['Month'] = data['Date'].dt.month
data['Month_Name'] = data['Date'].dt.strftime('%B')

# Calculate revenue per line item
data['Revenue'] = data['Sales Price Per Unit'] * data['Quantity']

# Group by month and calculate total revenue
monthly_revenue = data.groupby(['Month', 'Month_Name'])['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()
monthly_revenue = monthly_revenue.sort_values('Month')

# Print results
print("Monthly Revenue Analysis:")
for _, row in monthly_revenue.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['Month_Name']}: ${row['Revenue']:,.2f}")

# Find highest revenue month
highest_month = monthly_revenue.loc[monthly_revenue['Revenue'].idxmax()]
print(f"\nHighest Revenue Month: {highest_month['Month_Name']} with ${highest_month['Revenue']:,.2f}")

# Create visualization
fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Bar(x=monthly_revenue['Month_Name'], y=monthly_revenue['Revenue'], marker_color='steelblue')])
fig.update_layout(title='Monthly Revenue - First 6 Months 2013', xaxis_title='Month', yaxis_title='Revenue ($)')
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=45)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat='$,.0f')
fig.show()

# Answer the question
print(f"\nAnswer: {highest_month['Month_Name']}")

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date to datetime and filter for February 2013 and specified postal codes
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])
filtered_data = data[
    (data['Date'].dt.month == 2) & 
    (data['Date'].dt.year == 2013) & 
    (data['Postal Code'].isin([3013, 3017, 3031]))
]

# Calculate profit per line item
filtered_data = filtered_data.copy()
filtered_data['Profit'] = (filtered_data['Sales Price Per Unit'] - filtered_data['Cost per Unit']) * filtered_data['Quantity']

# Group by Item Code and sum total profit
item_profits = filtered_data.groupby('Item Code').agg({'Profit': 'sum'}).reset_index()
item_profits = item_profits.sort_values('Profit', ascending=False)

# Find item with highest profit
highest_profit_item = item_profits.iloc[0]['Item Code']
highest_profit_value = item_profits.iloc[0]['Profit']

print(f"Item with highest total profit in February for postal codes 3013, 3017, 3031: {int(highest_profit_item)}")
print(f"Total profit: ${highest_profit_value:.2f}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(item_profits.head(10), x='Item Code', y='Profit', 
             title='Top 10 Items by Total Profit - February 2013 (Postal Codes 3013, 3017, 3031)')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Item Code')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Profit ($)')
fig.show()

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date to datetime and extract month
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])
data['Month'] = data['Date'].dt.month
data['Month_Name'] = data['Date'].dt.strftime('%B')

# Calculate profit per line
data['Profit_Per_Line'] = (data['Sales Price Per Unit'] - data['Cost per Unit']) * data['Quantity']

# Group by month and calculate total profit
monthly_profit = data.groupby(['Month', 'Month_Name']).agg({'Profit_Per_Line': 'sum'}).reset_index()
monthly_profit = monthly_profit.sort_values('Profit_Per_Line', ascending=False)

# Print ranking
print("Monthly Profit Ranking (Highest to Lowest):")
for i, row in monthly_profit.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['Month_Name']}: ${row['Profit_Per_Line']:,.2f}")

ranking = ', '.join(monthly_profit['Month_Name'].tolist())
print(f"\nRanking: {ranking}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(monthly_profit, x='Month_Name', y='Profit_Per_Line', 
             title='Monthly Profit Ranking (Jan-Jun 2013)',
             labels={'Profit_Per_Line': 'Total Profit ($)', 'Month_Name': 'Month'})
fig.update_xaxes(title='Month')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Total Profit ($)', tickformat='$,.0f')
fig.show()

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from datetime import datetime

# Convert date and filter data
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])
filtered_data = data[
    (~data['Postal Code'].isin([3019, 3028])) & 
    (~data['Item Code'].isin([4, 5, 6, 17, 18])) & 
    (data['Manager On Duty'] == 'John Jones')
].copy()

# Calculate profit and extract month
filtered_data['Profit'] = (filtered_data['Sales Price Per Unit'] - filtered_data['Cost per Unit']) * filtered_data['Quantity']
filtered_data['Month'] = filtered_data['Date'].dt.month

# Group by month and calculate total profit
monthly_profit = filtered_data.groupby('Month')['Profit'].sum().reset_index()
monthly_profit['Cumulative_Profit'] = monthly_profit['Profit'].cumsum()
monthly_profit['Month_Name'] = monthly_profit['Month'].map({1:'Jan', 2:'Feb', 3:'Mar', 4:'Apr', 5:'May', 6:'Jun'})

# Find top 3 months
top_3 = monthly_profit.nlargest(3, 'Cumulative_Profit')['Month_Name'].tolist()
print(f"Top 3 months with highest cumulative profit: {', '.join(top_3)}")

# Visualization
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=monthly_profit['Month_Name'], y=monthly_profit['Cumulative_Profit'], mode='lines+markers', name='Cumulative Profit'))
fig.update_layout(title='John Jones - Monthly Cumulative Profit')
fig.update_xaxes(title='Month')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Cumulative Profit ($)')
fig.show()

dsagent_code:
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Convert Date column to datetime
data['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['Date'])

# Filter for June 2013, Item Code 3, and salespeople Benny and Kelly
june_filter = (data['Date'].dt.month == 6) & (data['Date'].dt.year == 2013)
item_filter = data['Item Code'] == 3
salesperson_filter = data['Sales Person'].isin(['Benny Bernanke', 'Kelly Clark'])

filtered_data = data[june_filter & item_filter & salesperson_filter]

# Calculate total quantity by salesperson
quantity_by_person = filtered_data.groupby('Sales Person')['Quantity'].sum()
total_quantity = quantity_by_person.sum()

print(f"Quantity of Item 3 sold in June 2013:")
for person, qty in quantity_by_person.items():
    print(f"{person}: {qty}")
print(f"Total quantity by Benny and Kelly: {total_quantity}")

# Create visualization
fig = px.bar(x=quantity_by_person.index, y=quantity_by_person.values,
             title='Item 3 Quantities Sold by Benny and Kelly in June 2013',
             labels={'x': 'Sales Person', 'y': 'Quantity Sold'})
fig.update_xaxes(title='Sales Person')
fig.update_yaxes(title='Quantity Sold')
fig.show()

print(f"\nAnswer: {total_quantity}")
